{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Admin Support Officer",
  "description": "Maurice Xandra Solutions Limited - Our client, a leading oil and gas company in Lagos State, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Admin Support Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Job Description


	Our client is seeking a highly experienced and proactive Admin Manager to oversee and optimize administrative operations across the organization.
	The ideal candidate will be a seasoned professional capable of coordinating office functions, managing vendors, and supporting executive operations efficiently and discreetly.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee day-to-day administrative operations of the organization
	Manage facilities operations, including security, maintenance, and housekeeping
	Handle procurement, vendor management, and contract administration
	Monitor and control office budgets, supplies, and operational costs
	Coordinate executive schedules, travel logistics, visas, and official engagements
	Ensure proper documentation, record-keeping, and filing systems
	Develop and implement administrative policies and process improvements
	Liaise with internal stakeholders and external service providers to ensure smooth operations


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	5&ndash;8 years of experience in administration, preferably within the oil &amp; gas or energy sector
	Proven experience managing administrative functions and operations
	Strong organizational, leadership, and problem-solving skills
	Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities
	Ability to work independently while maintaining professionalism
	Mature, composed, and highly professional
	Strong sense of ownership, accountability, and attention to detail
	Confident in handling executive-level responsibilities
	Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment


Experience:


	Administrative Management: Minimum 5 years (Required).


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@mauricexandra.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-02",

Admin Support Officer Job at Maurice Xandra Solutions Limited




        
    

        
        Maurice Xandra Solutions Limited - Our client, a leading oil and gas company in Lagos State, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Admin Support Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Job Description


	Our client is seeking a highly experienced and proactive Admin Manager to oversee and optimize administrative operations across the organization.
	The ideal candidate will be a seasoned professional capable of coordinating office functions, managing vendors, and supporting executive operations efficiently and discreetly.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Oversee day-to-day administrative operations of the organization
	Manage facilities operations, including security, maintenance, and housekeeping
	Handle procurement, vendor management, and contract administration
	Monitor and control office budgets, supplies, and operational costs
	Coordinate executive schedules, travel logistics, visas, and official engagements
	Ensure proper documentation, record-keeping, and filing systems
	Develop and implement administrative policies and process improvements
	Liaise with internal stakeholders and external service providers to ensure smooth operations


Requirements & Qualifications


	5–8 years of experience in administration, preferably within the oil & gas or energy sector
	Proven experience managing administrative functions and operations
	Strong organizational, leadership, and problem-solving skills
	Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities
	Ability to work independently while maintaining professionalism
	Mature, composed, and highly professional
	Strong sense of ownership, accountability, and attention to detail
	Confident in handling executive-level responsibilities
	Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment


Experience:


	Administrative Management: Minimum 5 years (Required).


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Account Manager (French Speaking)",
  "description": "Flutterwave was founded on the principle that every African must be able to participate and thrive in the global economy. To achieve this objective, we have built a trusted payment infrastructure that allows consumers and businesses (African and International) make and receive payments in a convenient border-less manner.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Account Manager (French Speaking)

Job Requisition ID:&nbsp;30095969
Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki, Lagos

Job Description


	We are looking for an Account Manager. This role will primarily be responsible for the management of the company&rsquo;s customers by overseeing the portfolios of assigned customers and building long-term mutually beneficial relationships between the customers and the company.


What Your Day-to-Day Activities Will Be Like&nbsp;


	Serve as the lead point of contact for all customer account management matters
	Build and maintain strong, long-lasting client relationships
	Ensure customers&rsquo; needs and demands are well communicated
	Ensure services are rendered and delivered in a timely fashion
	Forecasts and tracks key accounts metrics
	Manage projects within customer&rsquo;s relationships, working to carry out customer goals while meeting company goals
	Identify opportunities to grow business with existing customers
	Develop clear and trackable accounts plan for assigned merchants
	Monitor merchant fees and track margin to ensure profitability
	Communicate in a timely and clear manner, the progress of monthly/quarterly initiatives to internal and external stakeholders
	Prepare reports on account status
	Ensure that customer complaints are investigated and promptly resolved across all channels
	Act on behalf of; and perform any other duties that may be assigned by the Account Management Lead&nbsp;
	Ensure personal information of customers, employees, and other individuals the company conducts business with is processed and protected in line with applicable data privacy policies, privacy laws, and global best practices.


Requirements
Required competency and skillset to be a waver:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or equivalent experience strongly preferred
	4+ years of relevant work experience in customer service and customer experience
	Knowledge and understanding of Flutterwave&rsquo;s product and service offerings&nbsp;
	Proven expertise in fostering strong relationships with customers
	Efficient multi-tasker and&nbsp;ability&nbsp;to work &amp; deliver under pressure
	Excellent client management, communication, negotiation and problem-solving skills
	Experience creating and presenting reports to executive management
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite
	Authorization to work in Nigeria without sponsorship
	Professional fluency in french is required

Account Manager (French Speaking) Job at Flutterwave




        
    

        
        Flutterwave was founded on the principle that every African must be able to participate and thrive in the global economy. To achieve this objective, we have built a trusted payment infrastructure that allows consumers and businesses (African and International) make and receive payments in a convenient border-less manner.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Manager (French Speaking)

Job Requisition ID: 30095969
Job Location: Lekki, Lagos

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for an Account Manager. This role will primarily be responsible for the management of the company’s customers by overseeing the portfolios of assigned customers and building long-term mutually beneficial relationships between the customers and the company.


What Your Day-to-Day Activities Will Be Like 


	Serve as the lead point of contact for all customer account management matters
	Build and maintain strong, long-lasting client relationships
	Ensure customers’ needs and demands are well communicated
	Ensure services are rendered and delivered in a timely fashion
	Forecasts and tracks key accounts metrics
	Manage projects within customer’s relationships, working to carry out customer goals while meeting company goals
	Identify opportunities to grow business with existing customers
	Develop clear and trackable accounts plan for assigned merchants
	Monitor merchant fees and track margin to ensure profitability
	Communicate in a timely and clear manner, the progress of monthly/quarterly initiatives to internal and external stakeholders
	Prepare reports on account status
	Ensure that customer complaints are investigated and promptly resolved across all channels
	Act on behalf of; and perform any other duties that may be assigned by the Account Management Lead 
	Ensure personal information of customers, employees, and other individuals the company conducts business with is processed and protected in line with applicable data privacy policies, privacy laws, and global best practices.


Requirements
Required competency and skillset to be a waver:


	Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience strongly preferred
	4+ years of relevant work experience in customer service and customer experience
	Knowledge and understanding of Flutterwave’s product and service offerings 
	Proven expertise in fostering strong relationships with customers
	Efficient multi-tasker and ability to work & deliver under pressure
	Excellent client management, communication, negotiation and problem-solving skills
	Experience creating and presenting reports to executive management
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite
	Authorization to work in Nigeria without sponsorship
	Professional fluency in french is required
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Internal Control Executive",
  "description": "Guinea Insurance Plc was incorporated on December 3, 1958, as a non-life insurance underwriting firm to provide first-rate insurance solutions in the General and Special Risks business underwriting categories. Our mission is to provide professional services to our esteemed customers through the introduction of quality products driven by state-of-the-art technology backed by a group of high-profiled personnel, to maximize returns to shareholders.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Internal Control Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Internal Control Officer will support the implementation, monitoring, and improvement of the company&rsquo;s internal control framework.
	The role focuses on ensuring compliance with policies, identifying risks, and supporting control processes across various departments within Guinea Insurance Plc.
	The officer will work closely with teams to promote a strong control environment and efficient operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in the implementation and documentation of internal control policies and procedures.
	Support periodic reviews and basic audits to ensure compliance with internal policies and regulatory requirements.
	Help identify control gaps, process weaknesses, and potential risk areas, and escalate findings appropriately.
	Monitor selected financial and operational activities to detect irregularities or control breaches.
	Assist in preparing reports on audit findings, risk observations, and control issues for management review.
	Support control activities, including monitoring outstanding premiums and following up on claims process.
	Maintain proper documentation of control activities, audit work, and supporting records.
	Stay informed on relevant regulatory guidelines affecting the insurance industry.
	Collaborate with various departments to ensure adherence to established policies and procedures.
	Provide administrative and operational support during internal reviews and control assessments.


Qualification / Experience Requirement(s)


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in internal control and audit, preferably in the insurance or financial services industry.
	Relevant professional certification (e.g., ACA or its equivalent is an advantage).
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent attention to detail and ability to identify control weaknesses.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and audit-related software.
	Good communication and report-writing skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@guineainsurance.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-02",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

Internal Control Executive Job at Guinea Insurance Plc




        
    

        
        Guinea Insurance Plc was incorporated on December 3, 1958, as a non-life insurance underwriting firm to provide first-rate insurance solutions in the General and Special Risks business underwriting categories. Our mission is to provide professional services to our esteemed customers through the introduction of quality products driven by state-of-the-art technology backed by a group of high-profiled personnel, to maximize returns to shareholders.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Control Executive

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Internal Control Officer will support the implementation, monitoring, and improvement of the company’s internal control framework.
	The role focuses on ensuring compliance with policies, identifying risks, and supporting control processes across various departments within Guinea Insurance Plc.
	The officer will work closely with teams to promote a strong control environment and efficient operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in the implementation and documentation of internal control policies and procedures.
	Support periodic reviews and basic audits to ensure compliance with internal policies and regulatory requirements.
	Help identify control gaps, process weaknesses, and potential risk areas, and escalate findings appropriately.
	Monitor selected financial and operational activities to detect irregularities or control breaches.
	Assist in preparing reports on audit findings, risk observations, and control issues for management review.
	Support control activities, including monitoring outstanding premiums and following up on claims process.
	Maintain proper documentation of control activities, audit work, and supporting records.
	Stay informed on relevant regulatory guidelines affecting the insurance industry.
	Collaborate with various departments to ensure adherence to established policies and procedures.
	Provide administrative and operational support during internal reviews and control assessments.


Qualification / Experience Requirement(s)


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in internal control and audit, preferably in the insurance or financial services industry.
	Relevant professional certification (e.g., ACA or its equivalent is an advantage).
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent attention to detail and ability to identify control weaknesses.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and audit-related software.
	Good communication and report-writing skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Operations Manager",
  "description": "JNO Integrated Services is a dynamic HR consultancy firm dedicated to helping businesses build strong, efficient, and compliant workforces. We specialize in providing tailored HR solutions, including talent acquisition, employee training, performance management, and HR advisory services. Our team of experts is committed to aligning HR strategies with business goals, ensuring that our clients have the right talent and systems in place to thrive in today&rsquo;s competitive market.

At JNO Integrated Services, we understand the complexities of managing people and the importance of compliance with labor laws. We provide strategic guidance and operational support, empowering organizations to maximize employee potential and drive growth. Whether you&rsquo;re a small business looking to scale or a large corporation seeking to streamline HR processes, we&rsquo;re here to deliver results.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Operations Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Our client&nbsp;is seeking a qualified and experienced Senior Operations Manager to join their team.
	As a Senior Operations Manager, you will be responsible for managing the logistics, production and execution of experiential marketing events, activations and campaigns.


Key Responsibilities


	Creating cost saving measures in procurement activities.
	Ensuring executions are seamlessly in line with the client&rsquo;s operating standard.
	Recruitment of viable vendors to achieve bespoke and hitch free production elements.
	Standard activity reporting.
	Development of cutting-edge ideas, budget drafting and ensuring excellent executions.
	Project management capabilities resulting in brilliant executions.
	Provide strong leadership and effectively directs team members to accomplish desired goals.
	Drive operations in execution planning and ensure deliverables arefulfilled and timelines to client are kept.
	Evaluate the effectiveness of activations by monitoring feedback and obtaining quantifiable results.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Marketing, Business or related field.
	6+ years of experience in operations, event management or logistics.
	Proven track records of successful event execution and project management.
	Ability to work under pressure.
	Project management skills.
	Strong leadership and team management skills.
	Excellent communication, strategic thinking, and problem-solving skills.
	Experience in experiential marketing or a related industry.


Qualifications:


	Certification in project management is an advantage.
	Strong understanding of experiential marketing, events, or brand activation.
	Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills.
	Proven ability to manage multiple projects and Clients under pressure.
	Strong attention to detail.
	A creative thinker with a passion for live experiences.
	Candidate must hold at least a 2:1 degree or higher.


What We Offer


	Competitive Salary (N500,000 - N700,000 monthly)&nbsp;and benefits package.
	Opportunities for career development and training.
	A supportive and collaborative work environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to:&nbsp;recruitment.jnoservices@gmail.com&nbsp;using &ldquo;Senior Operations Manager&rdquo; as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-01",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-11",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

Senior Operations Manager Job at JNO Integrated Services




        
    

        
        JNO Integrated Services is a dynamic HR consultancy firm dedicated to helping businesses build strong, efficient, and compliant workforces. We specialize in providing tailored HR solutions, including talent acquisition, employee training, performance management, and HR advisory services. Our team of experts is committed to aligning HR strategies with business goals, ensuring that our clients have the right talent and systems in place to thrive in today’s competitive market.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



At JNO Integrated Services, we understand the complexities of managing people and the importance of compliance with labor laws. We provide strategic guidance and operational support, empowering organizations to maximize employee potential and drive growth. Whether you’re a small business looking to scale or a large corporation seeking to streamline HR processes, we’re here to deliver results.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Operations Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Our client is seeking a qualified and experienced Senior Operations Manager to join their team.
	As a Senior Operations Manager, you will be responsible for managing the logistics, production and execution of experiential marketing events, activations and campaigns.


Key Responsibilities


	Creating cost saving measures in procurement activities.
	Ensuring executions are seamlessly in line with the client’s operating standard.
	Recruitment of viable vendors to achieve bespoke and hitch free production elements.
	Standard activity reporting.
	Development of cutting-edge ideas, budget drafting and ensuring excellent executions.
	Project management capabilities resulting in brilliant executions.
	Provide strong leadership and effectively directs team members to accomplish desired goals.
	Drive operations in execution planning and ensure deliverables arefulfilled and timelines to client are kept.
	Evaluate the effectiveness of activations by monitoring feedback and obtaining quantifiable results.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Business or related field.
	6+ years of experience in operations, event management or logistics.
	Proven track records of successful event execution and project management.
	Ability to work under pressure.
	Project management skills.
	Strong leadership and team management skills.
	Excellent communication, strategic thinking, and problem-solving skills.
	Experience in experiential marketing or a related industry.


Qualifications:


	Certification in project management is an advantage.
	Strong understanding of experiential marketing, events, or brand activation.
	Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills.
	Proven ability to manage multiple projects and Clients under pressure.
	Strong attention to detail.
	A creative thinker with a passion for live experiences.
	Candidate must hold at least a 2:1 degree or higher.


What We Offer


	Competitive Salary (N500,000 - N700,000 monthly) and benefits package.
	Opportunities for career development and training.
	A supportive and collaborative work environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using “Senior Operations Manager” as the subject of the mail.
